steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary. Since angles $M$ and $N$ add to $90^\circ$, the sine of one equals the cosine of the other.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity
steps.content:
For complementary angles,
$$\sin(M)=\cos(N)$$
The problem gives
$$\sin(M)=\frac{\sqrt{5}}{3}$$
so
$$\cos(N)=\frac{\sqrt{5}}{3}$$
The answer is B.
